title: Best headphones I've ever owned
text: I've tried many wireless headphones over the years, but these are on a different level. The noise cancellation is outstanding — I use them on my daily commute and can't hear anything around me. Battery life easily lasts two full workdays. The sound is rich and well-balanced, with deep bass that doesn't overpower the mids. The ear cushions are incredibly comfortable even after 4-5 hours of use. Build quality feels premium. Highly recommend.

title: Exceptional sound quality for the price
text: For the price, these headphones punch way above their weight. The audio is crisp and detailed, and the noise cancellation actually works well in noisy offices. I was skeptical at first, but after a week of daily use I'm genuinely impressed. Pairing is instant, the app works perfectly, and battery life is solid. My only minor gripe is that the carrying case feels a bit cheap.

title: Life-changing noise cancellation
text: I work in an open-plan office and these headphones have genuinely improved my focus and productivity. The ANC blocks out conversations completely. Call quality is also excellent — people tell me I sound clearer than on my phone. Comfortable enough for all-day wear. 100% worth every penny.

title: Perfect travel companion
text: These fold flat, fit in any bag, and the battery lasts for 14-hour flights with ANC on. Sound quality is warm and pleasing. I've used them on six international trips and they haven't let me down once. Customer support also replaced a faulty cable within 48 hours, no questions asked.

title: Great headphones with minor Bluetooth quirks
text: Sound and comfort are both excellent — I especially love the transparency mode for when I need to hear my surroundings. Battery life is as advertised. The only reason I'm giving 4 stars is that Bluetooth occasionally drops for a split second when my phone is in my pocket. It's rare but annoying enough to mention. Everything else is top notch.

title: Very good, almost perfect
text: These are my daily drivers and I love them. The soundstage is wide and instrument separation is great for the price. My only complaints are that the touch controls are a bit oversensitive — I accidentally skip tracks when adjusting the fit — and the app could be more intuitive. But the audio quality is genuinely excellent.

title: Solid workout headphones
text: I use these exclusively for running and gym sessions. They stay in place, the IP55 rating gives me peace of mind when it rains, and the sound is energetic. My ears start to ache after about 90 minutes so I take breaks, but that might just be my ear shape. Battery life is good — around 8 hours per charge.

title: Decent but nothing special
text: These get the job done but feel average compared to similarly-priced competitors. The sound is okay — a bit bass-heavy for my taste. Noise cancellation exists but isn't impressive. Build quality feels solid enough. I'd call them reliable and inoffensive rather than exciting. Fine for casual use, not for audiophiles.

title: Good sound, poor mic quality
text: As headphones for music, these are genuinely good — warm, detailed, comfortable. But if you plan to use them for calls, look elsewhere. The microphone picks up a lot of ambient noise and people consistently say I sound muffled. For music-only use I'd rate them 4 stars, but the call quality drops my rating to 3.

title: Disappointed after two months
text: The headphones sounded great for the first month. Then the left cup started cutting out intermittently. It got progressively worse and now the left side drops out every few minutes. I contacted support but the warranty process has been a frustrating experience. For the price I expected better build quality and much better after-sales service.

title: Returned after a week
text: Bass is overwhelming and drowns out everything else. I tried adjusting the EQ in the app but couldn't get a balanced sound. They're also quite tight on the head, which became painful after an hour. The battery life was good and the case is nice, but the sound profile just doesn't suit me. Returned for a refund.

title: Broke after 3 weeks
text: The right earcup hinge snapped during normal folding. Three weeks of careful use and the headphones are unwearable. The audio quality was good while they lasted, which makes this even more frustrating. Quality control is clearly a problem. Do not buy.

title: Total waste of money
text: The noise cancellation produces a loud hissing sound that is more distracting than the ambient noise it's supposed to block. Returned immediately. Save your money.

title: Best USB-C cable I've found
text: I've gone through countless cheap cables that fray or lose their charge after a few months. This one has been going strong for over a year. The braided nylon is tough, the connectors feel solid, and it charges my laptop at full speed. The 2m length is perfect for desktop use. Bought three more for the rest of my devices.

title: Fast charging that actually works
text: This cable supports the full 100W PD spec and I've verified it charges my laptop at rated speed. Most cheap cables don't deliver on fast charging claims but this one genuinely does. Build quality is excellent — thick, flexible, no sharp bends at the connector. Highly recommended for anyone who wants reliable fast charging.

title: Exactly what a cable should be
text: It charges fast, it works with all my devices, it hasn't broken. What else do you want from a cable? The cable management strap is a nice touch. Bought two years ago and it still looks brand new.

title: Good cable, packaging excessive
text: Functionally this is a great cable — fast, durable, works with all my USB-C devices. I knocked off a star only because it comes in an enormous box with layers of plastic packaging for what is just a cable. The product itself is 5 stars.

title: Reliable daily driver
text: Using this cable to charge my phone, tablet, and laptop — it handles all three without issues. The 90-degree connector on one end is genuinely useful for when the phone is flat on a table. Only minor issue is that the cable is slightly stiff when cold, loosening up after a minute. No durability concerns after 8 months.

title: Works but heats up
text: The cable charges my devices fine but gets noticeably warm during fast charging. Not dangerously hot, but warmer than other cables I've used. I've dropped to using it for slower overnight charging rather than quick top-ups. It could just be my particular unit, but it makes me slightly uneasy.

title: Stopped working after 2 months
text: Worked perfectly for the first two months, then started cutting out during charging. The connection became unreliable — sometimes it charges, sometimes it doesn't, depending on the angle. Definitely a connector quality issue. Expected more longevity.

title: Damaged my port
text: The connector fit was extremely tight going in and when I pulled it out it brought some debris with it from my port. My phone now charges at reduced speed. Avoid this brand.

title: Changed how I work from home
text: This mechanical keyboard was an investment but completely worth it. The typing experience is satisfying in a way that's hard to describe — every keystroke feels precise and responsive. My typing speed has noticeably improved. The RGB is subtle and tasteful. Highly recommended for anyone who types a lot.

title: Perfect productivity keyboard
text: I write code for 8+ hours a day and this keyboard has eliminated the wrist discomfort I used to get with my old membrane keyboard. The tenkeyless layout gives me more room for the mouse, and the build is rock solid — no flex, no wobble. Excellent product.

title: Best keyboard under €100
text: I was skeptical about paying this much for a keyboard, but after two weeks I understand the hype. The switches are smooth and tactile, the aluminium frame adds real weight and stability, and the software lets you remap every key. Worth every cent.

title: Great keyboard, noisy switches
text: The keyboard itself is excellent — comfortable, well-built, responsive. But I chose clicky switches without fully understanding how loud they are. My colleagues in the office can hear me typing from across the room. That's entirely my fault for the switch choice. The keyboard itself deserves 5 stars; I'm rating 4 to account for the noise warning I wish I'd had.

title: Solid but software is buggy
text: Hardware is excellent — premium feel, comfortable typing, good actuation force. The software for customising lighting profiles is frustratingly buggy though. It crashed three times during setup and once wiped my custom profiles. Once configured it works fine, but getting there was painful. Better software would make this a 5-star product.

title: Decent keyboard, nothing more
text: It types fine and feels reasonably well-made, but I don't understand the glowing reviews. The keycaps are thin and feel slightly hollow. The software is basic. It does the job, but at this price point I expected something more impressive.

title: Keys started sticking after 6 months
text: The keyboard typed beautifully for the first few months. Then the G and H keys started requiring extra pressure to register. I cleaned it thoroughly but the problem persists. For daily use the keyboard is now unreliable. Disappointing build quality for the price.

title: Dead on arrival
text: One key column was completely unresponsive straight out of the box. Contacted support, waited 10 days for a response, and was told to ship it back at my own cost. Not acceptable for a product in this price range.

title: Incredibly precise mouse for work and gaming
text: The sensor on this mouse is flawless — zero acceleration, perfectly consistent tracking on any surface. The adjustable DPI range covers everything from photo editing to fast-paced gaming. Ergonomics are well thought out and it fits my large hands perfectly. After six months of daily use it feels as new as day one.

title: The best mouse I've used in 20 years of computing
text: I've used dozens of mice over my career. This one is simply the best. The scroll wheel has satisfying notches, the buttons are perfectly weighted, and the wireless connection has never dropped. Battery lasts three months on a charge. Effortless to recommend.

title: Great gaming mouse, charging annoyance
text: Performance is excellent — low-latency wireless, precise sensor, comfortable shape. The only thing I don't love is the charging: it uses a proprietary dock rather than USB-C, which means I need to remember to dock it rather than just plugging in a cable. Not a dealbreaker but a small inconvenience in 2025.

title: Comfortable with one caveat
text: Extremely comfortable for long sessions and the tracking is accurate. The side buttons are satisfyingly clicky. I dropped one star because the scroll wheel developed a slight squeak after three months. It doesn't affect functionality but it's annoying. Otherwise a very solid mouse.

title: Fine for the price, not for power users
text: If you want a basic mouse that just works, this is fine. The tracking is acceptable, the build is decent, and it's plug and play. But the DPI only goes to 1600, there's no software customisation, and the cable is a bit stiff. Does the job for everyday office use.

title: Scroll wheel died in 3 months
text: The mouse worked well initially but the scroll wheel became erratic after about three months. Scrolling would randomly jump to the top or bottom of the page. Replacement process was slow. I've since switched brands.

title: Terrible build quality
text: The left click button developed a double-click problem within a week. Every single click registers twice, making it impossible to use for anything precise. Known issue with this model based on other reviews. Avoid.

title: Fantastic webcam for remote work
text: Video quality is sharp and the autofocus is fast and accurate. My colleagues have commented on how much better I look in meetings compared to my old laptop camera. Low-light performance is genuinely impressive — well-lit even in my dim home office. The privacy shutter is a great feature. Plug and play on Mac and Windows.

title: Best webcam I've owned
text: 1080p at 60fps is silky smooth and the colour reproduction is natural. The built-in microphone is good enough that I stopped using my separate mic for most calls. Setup was instant. If you work from home, this is worth every penny.

title: Great image quality, software could improve
text: The hardware is excellent — sharp image, good dynamic range, reliable autofocus. The companion software for fine-tuning colour and exposure is functional but not polished. I'd also prefer the mount to have a wider range of tilt. But for day-to-day video calls these are minor complaints.

title: Average webcam at an above-average price
text: The image is okay in good lighting but degrades noticeably in lower light. For the price I expected better. The autofocus is also slow to lock on when you lean forward or back. It works, but I feel I overpaid slightly.

title: Constant driver issues on Windows 11
text: The image quality is good when it works, but I've had to reinstall drivers four times in two months. It randomly stops being recognised by Windows. Customer support sent me the same FAQ article twice. Not reliable enough for professional use.

title: Overheats and shuts off mid-call
text: The camera gets extremely hot during use and after about 30 minutes in a video call it simply shuts off. Completely unusable for back-to-back meetings. Returned immediately.

title: Eliminated cable chaos completely
text: I replaced eight separate adapters with this single hub and my desk has never been cleaner. All ports work at full spec — the HDMI outputs 4K60, USB-A ports deliver proper charging current, and the SD card reader is fast. It runs warm but never hot. Solid build, no driver issues in 8 months of daily use.

title: Essential for MacBook users
text: Apple removed all the ports and then sold us this. At least this hub is excellent — everything just works, the build quality matches the MacBook aesthetic, and the passthrough charging keeps my laptop fully powered. Highly recommended.

title: Good hub with one limitation
text: All ports work reliably and the build is solid. I knocked off one star because with four USB devices, an HDMI, and charging all connected simultaneously, the hub gets quite warm. Not alarming, but I'm conscious of it. Performance has been stable for six months.

title: Works but runs hot"
text: The hub delivers on its promise functionally — all ports work, speeds are as advertised. But it gets uncomfortably hot when heavily loaded. I've left it slightly elevated on a stand to improve airflow, which helps. Functional but I'd feel better if it ran cooler.

title: Two ports stopped working after a month
text: Started with six working ports, now down to four. The two USB-A ports on the left side simply stopped being recognised. Contacted support but haven't received a useful response after two weeks. Reliability is clearly an issue.

title: Fried my laptop port"
text: After two weeks of use the hub caused a charging fault that damaged my laptop's USB-C port. Repair cost more than the hub. I cannot in good conscience give this anything but one star.
